source: GitHub Scout Ecommerce Edition local seed data

summary.positioning: Merchant-first app savings and tech-layer intelligence for Shopify and BigCommerce owners.
summary.topSignal: Most ecommerce owners are overpaying for small widgets, app-stack duplication, and workflow tools that can often be replaced, simplified, or built once.

opportunities.name: Back-in-stock SMS alerts
opportunities.merchantOutcome: Recover demand when sold-out products come back.
opportunities.pain: Stores lose ready-to-buy shoppers when inventory returns but no one gets notified.
opportunities.reviewComplaint: Merchants complain that alerts get expensive once SMS and volume grow.
opportunities.freePath: Use a community notification workflow or lightweight app script tied to inventory webhooks.
opportunities.buildOnce: Developer needed, 2-4 hours for basic email alerts and 6-10 hours for SMS.
nextSteps: Export products with frequent stockouts.
Check whether your current restock app sends email, SMS, or both.
Test one free or build-once alert path for your top 10 products.

opportunities.name: Size guide builder
opportunities.merchantOutcome: Reduce fit questions and avoidable returns.
opportunities.pain: Sizing uncertainty creates support tickets, hesitates buyers, and increases returns.
opportunities.reviewComplaint: Common complaints mention styling conflicts and paying monthly for static tables.
opportunities.freePath: Add reusable theme sections or a metafield-powered size chart block.
opportunities.buildOnce: Theme editor only for simple charts; developer 1-2 hours for metafield automation.
nextSteps: List products with the most sizing questions.
Create one reusable chart block.
Measure return reason codes for 30 days.

opportunities.name: Bundle builder
opportunities.merchantOutcome: Lift average order value with simple bundles.
opportunities.pain: Merchants pay monthly for bundles that often only need a simple product group and discount rule.
opportunities.reviewComplaint: Merchants report edge cases with discounts, variants, and theme styling.
opportunities.freePath: Use native discounts plus a product-page bundle section.
opportunities.buildOnce: Developer 3-6 hours for a polished product-page widget.
nextSteps: Choose one natural 2-product bundle.
Create the discount rule.
Test bundle placement above and below add-to-cart.

opportunities.name: Product badge system
opportunities.merchantOutcome: Make best sellers, low-stock, and sale items easier to spot.
opportunities.pain: Stores pay for badge apps when most badge logic is simple theme logic.
opportunities.reviewComplaint: Merchants dislike paying monthly for visual labels that rarely change.
opportunities.freePath: Theme section or product metafield badges.
opportunities.buildOnce: Developer 1-3 hours for automatic badge rules.
nextSteps: Decide the only three badges you actually need.
Map badge rules to tags or metafields.
Replace badge app after a 7-day side-by-side test.

opportunities.name: Gift message widget
opportunities.merchantOutcome: Sell more gifts without adding checkout friction.
opportunities.pain: Gift-heavy stores need notes and packing instructions, but full gift-suite apps are often overkill.
opportunities.reviewComplaint: Merchants complain about setup weight when they only need a gift note field.
opportunities.freePath: Cart note field, line-item properties, or checkout extension depending on platform.
opportunities.buildOnce: Theme editor for cart notes; developer 2-5 hours for product-level messages.
nextSteps: Add one gift-note field.
Confirm it passes into orders and packing slips.
Track gift-note order rate during peak season.

opportunities.name: Age verification gate
opportunities.merchantOutcome: Meet basic compliance expectations without a bloated app.
opportunities.pain: Restricted-product stores often need a simple confirmation gate, not an expensive compliance suite.
opportunities.reviewComplaint: Merchants mention slow load, styling issues, and paying for a basic gate.
opportunities.freePath: Theme-level modal gate with cookie memory.
opportunities.buildOnce: Developer 2-4 hours; legal review still required for regulated categories.
nextSteps: Confirm your exact compliance need with counsel.
Replace simple gates only when legal requirements allow it.
Log date/version of any compliance copy changes.

opportunities.name: Returns portal lite
opportunities.pain: Small stores need a clear return form and status workflow before they need a full returns platform.
opportunities.reviewComplaint: Low-volume merchants often feel full returns suites are more platform than they need.
opportunities.freePath: Form plus tagged orders and canned email workflow.
opportunities.buildOnce: Developer 4-8 hours for a polished request form and admin workflow.
nextSteps: Count monthly return emails.
Create a single return request form.
Only buy a full returns suite after volume justifies automation.

opportunities.name: Wishlist lite
opportunities.merchantOutcome: Let shoppers save products without adding another heavy app.
opportunities.pain: Many wishlist apps are too heavy for stores that only need logged-out product saves.
opportunities.reviewComplaint: Merchants mention theme conflicts and paywalls around basic features.
opportunities.freePath: Browser-local wishlist saved in localStorage with simple email capture.
opportunities.buildOnce: Developer 3-6 hours for local wishlist and email reminder.
nextSteps: Decide whether logged-in wishlists are truly needed.
Test local saves first.
Add email reminder only after save volume is meaningful.

opportunities.name: Checkout trust widget
opportunities.merchantOutcome: Reduce hesitation near cart and checkout.
opportunities.pain: Stores add expensive trust apps when a few proof points and policy links are enough.
opportunities.reviewComplaint: Merchants complain about limited control and visual mismatch.
opportunities.freePath: Theme block for shipping, returns, guarantees, and payment icons.
opportunities.buildOnce: Theme editor for cart; developer needed for checkout extensibility rules.
nextSteps: Replace generic badges with real policy proof.
Place trust copy near cart actions.
Track cart-to-checkout rate after the change.

opportunities.name: Merchant analytics lite
opportunities.merchantOutcome: See which apps, widgets, and campaigns actually move money.
opportunities.pain: Owners drown in dashboards but still cannot tell which paid app is earning its keep.
opportunities.reviewComplaint: Merchants like the data but often struggle to turn it into app-stack decisions.
opportunities.freePath: Weekly spreadsheet or simple dashboard combining app spend, conversion, AOV, and support impact.
opportunities.buildOnce: Developer or analyst 4-10 hours for automated weekly reporting.
nextSteps: List every paid app and monthly cost.
Assign each app to revenue, conversion, support, compliance, or unknown.
Cancel or replace one unknown-value app this month.
